error.signature: ClusterBlockException: index [my_index] blocked by: [FORBIDDEN/12/index read-only / allow delete (api)]

verdict.summary: Elasticsearch set index to read-only because disk usage exceeded flood-stage watermark (95% by default).

dead_ends.action: Delete the index to free space
dead_ends.why_fails: You lose all data. Free disk space first, then unblock the index.
Only delays the problem. When disk is truly full, the cluster will crash.

workarounds.action: Free disk space, then unblock the index
workarounds.how: Free disk, then: PUT _all/_settings with index.blocks.read_only_allow_delete set to null

workarounds.action: Delete old indices using ILM or curator
workarounds.how: DELETE /old-index-2024.*  # or configure ILM delete phase

workarounds.action: Add more disk space or data nodes
